MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jul-31-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Scott Berman (scott@bermannorth.com) of Berman North, LLP for Plaintiff Pointer Labs, LLC.
Christopher J. Schweickert, Esq. (925.788.1672; cjs@wcjuris.com) of SETO WOOD & SCHWEICKERT LLP for Moving parties and defendants Remo Greco.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs REMO GRECO and BARRINGTON CAPITAL INVESTMENTS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS:

Defendants Barrington Capital Investments and Remo Greco's "motion to quash service of summons for lack of jurisdiction under CCP 418.10(a)(1) and alternatively motion to dismiss or stay under CCP 410.30 and 418.10(a)(2)" are denied.

Consulting agreements that defendants entered into - indeed, presented to plaintiff (Patel Dec. 4) - designate Northern California as the forum for any legal dispute. Such forum-selection clauses are consent to jurisdiction. (Nobel Farms, Inc. v. Pasero (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 654, 658.)

CCP 413.10(c) provides that service of summons is effective outside the United States when it satisfies California law, which law allows, as here, service to be made on lawyers authorized to accept it. (Ruszovan v. Ruszovan (1969) 268 Cal.App.2d 902, 906-07.)
order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
